summary: Tune and maintain the Application Load Balancers: target-group health checks, idle timeout, deregistration delay, listener-rule add/delete, and ALB tagging.

operations.title: Adjust target group health check thresholds and timing
operations.summary: Retune how often and how strictly a target group checks server health. Overly aggressive values can make healthy targets flap in and out.
operations.description: Tunes the health_check block (interval, timeout, healthy/unhealthy thresholds, matcher) on a target group; overly aggressive values can cause targets to flap in and out of service.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group whose health check will be retuned.

params.name: new_healthy_threshold
params.label: Healthy threshold (consecutive checks)
params.help: Number of consecutive successful health checks before an unhealthy target is put back in service; the 2-10 range matches AWS's valid healthy_threshold values, so a target can neither return on a single lucky check nor be held out indefinitely.

params.name: new_unhealthy_threshold
params.label: Unhealthy threshold (consecutive checks)
params.help: Number of consecutive failed health checks before a target is marked unhealthy and removed from rotation; the 2-10 range matches AWS's valid unhealthy_threshold values, so one transient blip can never eject a healthy target.

params.name: new_interval
params.label: Check interval (seconds)
params.help: Seconds between health checks.

params.name: new_timeout
params.label: Check timeout (seconds)
params.help: Must be less than the interval.

params.name: new_matcher
params.label: Success matcher
params.help: HTTP status code or range considered healthy, e.g. 200 or 200-299.

operations.title: Set ALB idle timeout
operations.summary: Set how long a connection can sit idle before the load balancer closes it. Too short a value cuts off long-running requests early.
operations.description: Changes idle_timeout on the load balancer; too low a value can prematurely close long-running application sessions, too high delays connection recycling.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to update.

params.name: new_idle_timeout_seconds
params.label: Idle timeout (seconds)
params.help: Seconds a connection may sit idle before the load balancer closes it (AWS attribute idle_timeout, default 60); the 1-4000 bound is exactly the range AWS accepts, so no value can be submitted that the API would reject.

operations.title: Set target group deregistration delay
operations.summary: Set how long the load balancer drains a removed target's connections. Shortening it speeds deploys but can drop in-flight requests.
operations.description: Changes deregistration_delay (connection draining period) on a target group; shortening it speeds up deploys but can drop in-flight requests from targets being removed.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group whose deregistration (connection-draining) delay will change, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed target group can be selected.

params.name: new_deregistration_delay
params.label: Deregistration delay (seconds)
params.help: Seconds the load balancer keeps draining in-flight requests to a target after deregistration begins (AWS attribute deregistration_delay, default 300); the 0-3600 bound is AWS's full valid range, where 0 drops connections immediately and 3600 is the longest drain AWS supports.

operations.title: Add a listener rule
operations.summary: Add a routing rule to a listener that sends matching traffic to a target group. A routing mistake can misdirect live production traffic.
operations.description: Creates a new aws_lb_listener_rule on an existing listener that forwards matching traffic (path pattern and/or host header) to a target group; a routing mistake can misdirect production traffic.

params.name: listener
params.label: Listener
params.help: Listener the new rule attaches to.

params.name: priority
params.label: Rule priority
params.help: Lower numbers are evaluated first; must be unique on the listener.

params.name: path_pattern
params.label: Path pattern condition
params.help: e.g. /PI/* — leave default to match all paths.

params.name: host_header
params.label: Host header condition
params.help: Optional hostname condition, e.g. app.internal.example.com.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Forward to target group
params.help: Target group that receives traffic matching this rule's conditions, chosen from the estate inventory so the rule can only forward to a Terraform-managed target group.

operations.title: Delete a listener rule
operations.summary: Permanently delete a listener rule. This can't be undone; its traffic falls through to the next rule. An engineer reviews it first.
operations.description: Removes an existing listener rule, permanently withdrawing its routing condition; any traffic depending on it will fall through to the next matching rule or the listener default action.

params.name: listener_rule
params.label: Listener rule
params.help: The listener rule to permanently remove, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed rule known to the repo can be deleted through this form.

operations.title: Add a tag to a load balancer
operations.summary: Add a tag to a load balancer for cost tracking or ownership. Metadata only, no effect on traffic.
operations.description: Adds a key/value pair to the ALB's tags map for cost allocation or ownership tracking; purely metadata with no effect on traffic.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: Load balancer that receives the new tag, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed ALB can be modified; tagging is metadata only and never affects traffic.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: e.g. CostCentre, Owner, Environment.

params.name: tag_value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: Text stored under the tag key, such as a cost-centre code or owner name; the 1-255 character bound stays inside AWS's 256-character tag-value limit, and tag values never affect load balancer behaviour.

operations.title: Toggle TLS version and cipher suite headers
operations.summary: Turn on or off extra headers that tell targets which TLS version and cipher the client used. Purely informational, no behavior change.
operations.description: Set enable_tls_version_and_cipher_suite_headers on the load balancer, which adds the x-amzn-tls-version and x-amzn-tls-cipher-suite headers to requests forwarded to targets. Application load balancers only; purely additive informational headers with no change to existing behavior.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to update.

params.name: enabled
params.label: TLS version/cipher headers enabled
params.help: Boolean; provider default is false (application type only).

operations.title: Set DNS record client routing policy
operations.summary: Set how DNS spreads traffic across this load balancer's Availability Zones. Network load balancers only.
operations.description: Set dns_record_client_routing_policy, controlling how traffic is distributed among the load balancer's Availability Zones at the DNS level. Network load balancers only.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to update.

params.name: policy
params.label: DNS record client routing policy
params.help: Doc enum; default any_availability_zone (network type only).

operations.title: Set IPv6 source NAT prefix mode
operations.summary: Turn on or off using a per-subnet IPv6 prefix for source address translation. Requires the load balancer to be dual-stack.
operations.description: Set enable_prefix_for_ipv6_source_nat (on/off), controlling whether an IPv6 prefix from each subnet is used for source NAT. Requires ip_address_type = dualstack.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to update.

params.name: mode
params.label: IPv6 source NAT prefix (on/off)
params.help: Doc enum on/off; requires ip_address_type = dualstack.

operations.title: Set X-Forwarded-For header processing mode
operations.summary: Set how the load balancer rewrites the client-IP header before forwarding requests. Backends that rely on client IP may be affected.
operations.description: Set xff_header_processing_mode (append/preserve/remove), controlling how the ALB modifies the X-Forwarded-For header before forwarding to targets. Application load balancers only; backends doing IP-based logic may be affected.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to update.

params.name: mode
params.label: XFF header processing mode
params.help: Doc enum; default append (application type only).

operations.title: Set client keep-alive duration
operations.summary: Set how long the load balancer keeps a client connection open. Too short a value can abruptly drop long-running client connections.
operations.description: Set client_keep_alive (seconds) on the load balancer. Too-short values abruptly drop active long-running client connections.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to update.

params.name: seconds
params.label: Client keep-alive (seconds)
params.help: Doc range 60-604800 seconds; provider default 3600.

operations.title: Toggle dropping of invalid HTTP header fields
operations.summary: Turn on or off dropping requests with invalid HTTP header fields instead of forwarding them. A security-hardening toggle.
operations.description: Set drop_invalid_header_fields, controlling whether HTTP headers with invalid field names are dropped instead of routed to targets. Application load balancers only; a hardening toggle.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to update.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Drop invalid header fields
params.help: Boolean; provider default false (application type only).

operations.title: Toggle load balancer cross-zone load balancing
operations.summary: Turn on or off spreading traffic evenly across all Availability Zones for this load balancer. Network and gateway types only.
operations.description: Set enable_cross_zone_load_balancing on the load balancer itself (distinct from alb-set-load-balancing-cross-zone, which sets the target group's load_balancing_cross_zone_enabled). Network/Gateway load balancers only — for application load balancers this is always enabled and cannot be changed.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to update.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Cross-zone load balancing enabled
params.help: Boolean; default false for network/gateway; locked true for application type.

operations.title: Toggle HTTP/2 support
operations.summary: Turn HTTP/2 on or off for this load balancer. Disabling forces every client back to HTTP/1.1.
operations.description: Set enable_http2, controlling whether HTTP/2 is enabled on the application load balancer. Applied LB-wide across all listeners; disabling forces clients to HTTP/1.1.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to update.

params.name: enabled
params.label: HTTP/2 enabled
params.help: Boolean; provider default true (application type only).

operations.title: Toggle X-Forwarded-For client port preservation
operations.summary: Turn on or off including the client's source port in the client-IP header sent to targets. Changes the format backends must parse.
operations.description: Set enable_xff_client_port, controlling whether the X-Forwarded-For header preserves the client's source port. Application load balancers only; changes the header format backends parse for client IP.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to update.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Preserve client port in XFF
params.help: Boolean; provider default false (application type only).

operations.title: Toggle zonal shift
operations.summary: Turn on or off the ability to shift traffic away from an Availability Zone during an incident. A resiliency safeguard.
operations.description: Set enable_zonal_shift, controlling whether zonal shift (AZ traffic shift during incidents) is enabled on the load balancer. A resiliency capability toggle.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to update.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Zonal shift enabled
params.help: Boolean; provider default false.

operations.title: Toggle Host header preservation
operations.summary: Turn on or off keeping the original Host header when forwarding to targets. Backends that route by hostname may depend on this.
operations.description: Set preserve_host_header, controlling whether the ALB preserves the incoming Host header when forwarding to targets. Backends doing vhost/routing logic may depend on this.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to update.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Preserve Host header
params.help: Boolean; provider default false.

operations.title: Enable or disable access logs
operations.summary: Turn access logging on or off. Enabling needs a log bucket already set up; disabling stops that audit trail.
operations.description: Toggle access_logs.enabled on the load balancer's access_logs block. Enabling requires an access_logs bucket to already be configured; disabling stops delivery of the S3 access-log audit trail.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to update.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Access logs enabled
params.help: Boolean; provider default false even when a bucket is set.

operations.title: Enable or disable connection logs
operations.summary: Turn connection logging on or off. Enabling needs a log bucket already set up; disabling stops that audit trail.
operations.description: Toggle connection_logs.enabled. Enabling requires a connection_logs bucket to already be configured; disabling stops delivery of the S3 connection-log audit trail. Application load balancers only.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to update.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Connection logs enabled
params.help: Boolean; provider default false even when a bucket is set.

operations.title: Enable or disable health check logs
operations.summary: Turn health-check logging on or off. Enabling needs a log bucket already set up; disabling stops that audit trail.
operations.description: Toggle health_check_logs.enabled. Enabling requires a health_check_logs bucket to already be configured; disabling stops delivery of the S3 health-check-log audit trail. Application load balancers only.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to update.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Health check logs enabled
params.help: Boolean; provider default false even when a bucket is set.

operations.title: Toggle deletion protection
operations.summary: Turn deletion protection on or off. Disabling removes the safeguard against deleting this load balancer. An engineer reviews it first.
operations.description: Set enable_deletion_protection. Disabling removes the guardrail that blocks deletion of the load balancer via the AWS API and Terraform.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to modify.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Deletion protection enabled
params.help: Boolean; provider default false.

operations.title: Toggle WAF fail-open
operations.summary: Set whether traffic bypasses WAF inspection if AWS WAF becomes unreachable. A security trade-off; an engineer reviews it first.
operations.description: Set enable_waf_fail_open, controlling whether a WAF-associated load balancer routes requests when it cannot reach AWS WAF. Enabling lets traffic bypass WAF inspection during a WAF outage.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to modify.

params.name: enabled
params.label: WAF fail-open enabled
params.help: Boolean; provider default false.

operations.title: Set security-group enforcement on PrivateLink traffic
operations.summary: Turn on or off enforcing security-group inbound rules for PrivateLink traffic. A security boundary change; an engineer reviews it first.
operations.description: Set enforce_security_group_inbound_rules_on_private_link_traffic (on/off), controlling whether inbound security-group rules are enforced for PrivateLink-originated traffic. Network load balancers only; a security-group boundary control.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to modify.

params.name: mode
params.label: Enforce SG inbound rules on PrivateLink (on/off)
params.help: Doc enum on/off (network type only).

operations.title: Set load balancer internal/internet-facing scheme
operations.summary: Switch between internal and internet-facing. Applying REPLACES the load balancer: new DNS name and a brief outage.
operations.description: Set `internal` (internet-facing to internal or back). CAUTION: `internal` is ForceNew on aws_lb — Terraform REPLACES the load balancer: new ARN and DNS name, and every listener and target-group attachment is recreated. Consumers of the old DNS name break until repointed. Engineer-authored change.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to modify.

params.name: internal
params.label: Internal (true) / internet-facing (false)
params.help: Boolean; provider default false (internet-facing).

operations.downtime: Brief outage while the load balancer is replaced; the DNS name changes, so consumers must repoint

operations.title: Set IP address type
operations.summary: Change which IP address families, including public IPv6, this load balancer accepts. A public-exposure change; an engineer reviews it first.
operations.description: Set ip_address_type, controlling which IP address families (including public IPv6) are reachable on the load balancer. A public-access exposure change with LB-wide blast radius.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to modify.

params.name: ip_address_type
params.label: IP address type
params.help: Doc enum; dualstack-without-public-ipv4 is application type only.

operations.title: Set load balancer type
operations.summary: Change the load balancer type: application, network, or gateway. Applying REPLACES it: new DNS name, brief outage, everything recreated.
operations.description: Set `load_balancer_type` (application/network/gateway). CAUTION: ForceNew on aws_lb — Terraform REPLACES the load balancer entirely (new ARN and DNS name, all listeners and attachments recreated). Engineer-authored change.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to modify.

params.name: load_balancer_type
params.label: Load balancer type
params.help: Doc enum; provider default application.

operations.downtime: Brief outage while the load balancer is replaced; the DNS name changes, so consumers must repoint

operations.title: Set secondary IPs auto-assigned per subnet
operations.summary: Set how many secondary IPs are auto-assigned per subnet (network load balancers). Decreasing this value REPLACES it, with a brief outage.
operations.description: Set secondary_ips_auto_assigned_per_subnet (0-7) on a network load balancer. Per the doc, decreasing this value forces full replacement of the load balancer.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to modify.

params.name: count
params.label: Secondary IPs per subnet (0-7)
params.help: Doc range 0-7; default 0 (network type only). Decreasing forces replacement.

operations.title: Rename the load balancer
operations.summary: Rename the load balancer. Applying REPLACES it: new DNS name, brief outage, and old records or hardcoded references break.
operations.description: Set name (max 32 chars, alphanumeric and hyphens, no leading or trailing hyphen). The load balancer's AWS-assigned DNS name is derived from this value; changing it breaks downstream DNS records and hardcoded references.

params.name: alb
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: ALB to rename.

params.name: name
params.label: Load balancer name
params.help: Max 32 chars, alphanumeric/hyphens, no leading/trailing hyphen (doc constraint).

operations.title: Add a tag to a target group
operations.summary: Add a tag to a target group for cost tracking or ownership. Metadata only, no effect on traffic.
operations.description: Adds a key/value pair to an aws_lb_target_group's tags map for cost allocation or ownership tracking; purely metadata with no effect on traffic.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to tag.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: e.g. CostCentre, Owner, Environment.

params.name: tag_value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: The tag value (1–128 characters).

operations.title: Set target group connection termination
operations.summary: Set whether the load balancer cuts a target's connections at the end of the drain period instead of letting them finish. Network type only.
operations.description: Sets connection_termination on an NLB target group, controlling whether the load balancer terminates connections at the end of the deregistration timeout instead of letting them drain.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to modify.

params.name: connection_termination
params.label: Terminate connections at deregistration timeout
params.help: Boolean; default false. NLB target groups only.

operations.title: Enable or disable target group health checks
operations.summary: Turn target group health checks on or off. Disabling removes failure detection, so an unhealthy target can stay in rotation.
operations.description: Sets health_check.enabled; disabling health checks removes failure detection for this target group so unhealthy targets stay in rotation.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to modify.

params.name: health_check_enabled
params.label: Health checks enabled
params.help: Boolean; default true.

operations.title: Set target group health check port
operations.summary: Set the port used to health-check this target group's servers. A wrong port marks healthy targets unhealthy and removes capacity.
operations.description: Sets health_check.port, the port the load balancer uses to health-check targets; a wrong port marks targets unhealthy and removes capacity.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to modify.

params.name: health_check_port
params.label: Health check port
params.help: 'traffic-port' (default) or a port number 1-65536.

operations.title: Set target group health check protocol
operations.summary: Set the protocol used to health-check this target group. A mismatch with what the targets actually speak marks them unhealthy.
operations.description: Sets health_check.protocol; a protocol mismatch with the targets causes health checks to fail and marks targets unhealthy.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to modify.

params.name: health_check_protocol
params.label: Health check protocol
params.help: Default HTTP. TCP is not supported if the target group protocol is HTTP/HTTPS; not applicable for lambda targets.

operations.title: Set Lambda multi-value headers
operations.summary: Change the request and response format sent to Lambda targets. If the function isn't updated to match, requests through it can fail.
operations.description: Sets lambda_multi_value_headers_enabled, which changes the request/response event format sent to Lambda targets; if the function is not updated to match, requests through this target group can fail.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to modify.

params.name: lambda_multi_value_headers_enabled
params.label: Lambda multi-value headers enabled
params.help: Boolean; default false. Only applies when target_type is lambda.

operations.title: Set target group load balancing anomaly mitigation
operations.summary: Turn on or off automatic mitigation for targets behaving abnormally. Only works with the weighted-random load-balancing algorithm.
operations.description: Sets load_balancing_anomaly_mitigation on/off, a reliability toggle that enables automated mitigation for anomalous targets. Only supported with the weighted_random algorithm.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to modify.

params.name: load_balancing_anomaly_mitigation
params.label: Anomaly mitigation
params.help: Default off. Only valid when the load balancing algorithm is weighted_random.

operations.title: Set target group client IP preservation
operations.summary: Turn on or off passing the real client IP through to targets. Changing it can break backend logic that depends on the source address.
operations.description: Sets preserve_client_ip, controlling whether targets see the original client IP; changing it can break backend logic that depends on the source address.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to modify.

params.name: preserve_client_ip
params.label: Preserve client IP
params.help: Boolean; provider default depends on target group type/protocol.

operations.title: Set target group proxy protocol v2
operations.summary: Turn on or off Proxy Protocol v2 between the load balancer and its targets. If targets aren't ready for it, connections break immediately.
operations.description: Sets proxy_protocol_v2 on an NLB target group, which changes the wire format between the load balancer and targets; if targets are not configured to expect the proxy protocol header, connections break immediately.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to modify.

params.name: proxy_protocol_v2
params.label: Proxy protocol v2 enabled
params.help: Boolean; default false. NLB target groups only.

operations.title: Set target group slow start duration
operations.summary: Set the warm-up period that gradually ramps up traffic to a newly registered target. Disabling or shortening it can overload new targets.
operations.description: Sets slow_start, the warm-up ramp during which a newly registered target receives a linearly increasing share of traffic; disabling or shortening it can overload new targets during deploys.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to modify.

params.name: slow_start_seconds
params.label: Slow start duration (seconds)
params.help: 0 disables slow start; otherwise 30-900 seconds (values 1-29 are rejected by AWS). Default 0.

operations.title: Enable or disable target group stickiness
operations.summary: Turn session stickiness on or off for this target group. Toggling it can break session continuity for users mid-session.
operations.description: Sets stickiness.enabled, turning session affinity on or off; toggling it can drop session continuity for active users mid-session.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to modify.

params.name: stickiness_enabled
params.label: Stickiness enabled
params.help: Boolean; default true.

operations.title: Set target group stickiness type
operations.summary: Change how this target group computes session stickiness. Switching the mechanism can disrupt users with an active session.
operations.description: Sets stickiness.type, the sticky-routing mechanism; changing it entirely alters how session affinity is computed and can disrupt active sessions.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to modify.

params.name: stickiness_type
params.label: Stickiness type
params.help: lb_cookie/app_cookie (ALB), source_ip (NLB), source_ip_dest_ip and source_ip_dest_ip_proto (GWLB).

operations.title: Set target group stickiness cookie duration
operations.summary: Set how long the stickiness cookie keeps a client routed to the same target. Shortening it a lot can drop affinity for active users.
operations.description: Sets stickiness.cookie_duration (used with type lb_cookie), the period a client keeps routing to the same target; shortening it drastically can drop session affinity for active users.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to modify.

params.name: cookie_duration_seconds
params.label: Cookie duration (seconds)
params.help: 1 second to 1 week (604800). Default 86400 (1 day). Only used when stickiness type is lb_cookie.

operations.title: Set target group failover flow handling
operations.summary: Set whether existing network flows are rebalanced when a target is removed or turns unhealthy. Gateway load balancer target groups only.
operations.description: Sets both target_failover.on_deregistration and target_failover.on_unhealthy on a GWLB target group to control how existing flows are handled when a target is deregistered or becomes unhealthy. AWS requires both attributes to be equal, so this operation sets them together.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to modify.

params.name: flow_rebalancing
params.label: Existing-flow handling (deregistration and unhealthy)
params.help: Default no_rebalance. Applied to both on_deregistration and on_unhealthy, which the provider requires to match. GWLB target groups only.

params.name: flow_rebalancing
params.label: Existing-flow handling (deregistration and unhealthy)
params.help: Default no_rebalance. Applied to both on_deregistration and on_unhealthy, which the provider requires to match. GWLB target groups only.

operations.title: Set termination of connections to unhealthy targets
operations.summary: Set whether client connections are cut the instant a target turns unhealthy, or left to keep hitting it. Network target groups only.
operations.description: Sets target_health_state.enable_unhealthy_connection_termination on an NLB (TCP/TLS) target group; disabling it lets clients keep hitting a target that has become unhealthy.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to modify.

params.name: enable_unhealthy_connection_termination
params.label: Terminate connections to unhealthy targets
params.help: Boolean; default true. NLB target groups with protocol TCP/TLS only.

operations.title: Set unhealthy target draining interval
operations.summary: Set how long in-flight requests may finish after a target turns unhealthy. Only applies when connections aren't cut immediately.
operations.description: Sets target_health_state.unhealthy_draining_interval, the time in-flight requests are allowed to complete when a target becomes unhealthy. Only meaningful when enable_unhealthy_connection_termination is false.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to modify.

params.name: unhealthy_draining_interval_seconds
params.label: Unhealthy draining interval (seconds)
params.help: 0-360000 seconds. Default 0. Applies only when enable_unhealthy_connection_termination is false.

operations.title: Set DNS failover minimum healthy target count
operations.summary: Set how many healthy targets are needed before an Availability Zone is marked unhealthy for DNS failover.
operations.description: Sets target_group_health.dns_failover.minimum_healthy_targets_count, the number of healthy targets required before the Availability Zone is marked unhealthy in DNS (Route 53 failover).

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to modify.

params.name: minimum_healthy_targets_count
params.label: Minimum healthy targets (count)
params.help: 'off' (default) or a positive integer up to the number of registered targets.

operations.title: Set DNS failover minimum healthy target percentage
operations.summary: Set what percentage of targets must be healthy before an Availability Zone is marked unhealthy for DNS failover.
operations.description: Sets target_group_health.dns_failover.minimum_healthy_targets_percentage, the percentage of healthy targets required before the Availability Zone is marked unhealthy in DNS (Route 53 failover).

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to modify.

params.name: minimum_healthy_targets_percentage
params.label: Minimum healthy targets (percentage)
params.help: 'off' (default) or an integer 1-100.

operations.title: Set unhealthy-state routing minimum healthy target count
operations.summary: Set the healthy-target count below which the load balancer routes to every target, including unhealthy ones, as a last resort.
operations.description: Sets target_group_health.unhealthy_state_routing.minimum_healthy_targets_count, the number of healthy targets below which traffic is sent to all targets (including unhealthy ones) as a last resort.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to modify.

params.name: minimum_healthy_targets_count
params.label: Minimum healthy targets (count)
params.help: A positive integer (1 to the number of registered targets). Default 1.

operations.title: Set unhealthy-state routing minimum healthy target percentage
operations.summary: Set the healthy-target percentage below which the load balancer routes to every target, including unhealthy ones, as a last resort.
operations.description: Sets target_group_health.unhealthy_state_routing.minimum_healthy_targets_percentage, the percentage of healthy targets below which traffic is sent to all targets (including unhealthy ones) as a last resort.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to modify.

params.name: minimum_healthy_targets_percentage
params.label: Minimum healthy targets (percentage)
params.help: 'off' (default) or an integer 1-100.

operations.title: Set target group protocol (forces replacement)
operations.summary: Change the target group's protocol. Applying REPLACES it: targets must re-register and listener rules re-point, with a brief interruption.
operations.description: Sets protocol on the target group. This is a ForceNew argument: changing it destroys and recreates the target group, requiring targets to re-register and listener rules to be re-pointed.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to replace.

params.name: protocol
params.label: Protocol
params.help: Required when target_type is instance, ip, or alb; not applicable for lambda.

operations.title: Set target group protocol version (forces replacement)
operations.summary: Change the target group's protocol version. Applying REPLACES it and can break protocol compatibility with targets; brief interruption.
operations.description: Sets protocol_version on the target group. This is a ForceNew argument: changing GRPC/HTTP2/HTTP1 destroys and recreates the target group and can break target protocol compatibility.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to replace.

params.name: protocol_version
params.label: Protocol version
params.help: Default HTTP1. Only applicable when protocol is HTTP or HTTPS.

operations.title: Set target group target type (forces replacement)
operations.summary: Change what kind of targets this group holds: instances, IPs, Lambda, or another load balancer. Applying REPLACES it; brief interruption.
operations.description: Sets target_type on the target group. This is a ForceNew argument: changing the fundamental target type destroys and recreates the target group.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to replace.

params.name: target_type
params.label: Target type
params.help: Default instance. NLBs do not support lambda; ALBs do not support alb targets.

operations.title: Set target group IP address type (forces replacement)
operations.summary: Change the target group's IP address type. Applying REPLACES it, causing a brief interruption.
operations.description: Sets ip_address_type on the target group. This is a ForceNew argument: changing the target IP addressing destroys and recreates the target group.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to replace.

params.name: ip_address_type
params.label: IP address type
params.help: Only applicable when target_type is ip.

operations.title: Set target group name (forces replacement)
operations.summary: Rename the target group. Applying REPLACES it: targets must re-register and listener rules re-point, with a brief interruption.
operations.description: Sets name on the target group. This is a ForceNew argument: renaming destroys and recreates the target group, requiring targets to re-register and listener rules to be re-pointed.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group to replace.

params.name: name
params.label: New target group name
params.help: Max 32 characters, alphanumeric and hyphens only, cannot begin or end with a hyphen. Must be unique per region/account.

operations.title: Add a tag to a listener rule
operations.summary: Add a tag to a listener rule for ownership or cost tracking. Metadata only, no effect on routing.
operations.description: Adds a key/value entry to an aws_lb_listener_rule's tags map for ownership or cost-allocation tracking; purely metadata with no effect on routing. Mirrors the existing alb-add-tag operation applied to this resource type.

params.name: listener_rule
params.label: Listener rule
params.help: Listener rule to tag.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: e.g. Owner, CostCentre, Environment.

params.name: tag_value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: The tag value (1–128 characters).

operations.title: Set listener rule fixed-response content type
operations.summary: Set the content type of the fixed response this listener rule returns, such as plain text or JSON.
operations.description: Changes action.fixed_response.content_type on a rule whose action already returns a fixed response; selects the MIME type of the static response body.

params.name: listener_rule
params.label: Listener rule
params.help: Listener rule whose fixed-response action to update.

params.name: content_type
params.label: Content type
params.help: MIME type of the fixed response body returned to the client.

operations.title: Set listener rule fixed-response status code
operations.summary: Set the HTTP status code this listener rule's fixed response returns, such as 200, 404, or 503.
operations.description: Changes action.fixed_response.status_code on a rule whose action already returns a fixed response; must be a three-digit HTTP code in the 2XX, 4XX or 5XX range (AWS API pattern ^(2|4|5)\d\d$).

params.name: listener_rule
params.label: Listener rule
params.help: Listener rule whose fixed-response action to update.

params.name: status_code
params.label: Status code
params.help: Three-digit HTTP code starting with 2, 4, or 5 (e.g. 200, 404, 503).

operations.title: Set listener rule redirect protocol
operations.summary: Set the protocol this listener rule redirects to. Choosing HTTP downgrades users from an encrypted connection on their next request.
operations.description: Changes action.redirect.protocol on a rule whose action already redirects. Setting HTTP downgrades users from an encrypted connection on their next request; #{protocol} preserves the incoming protocol.

params.name: listener_rule
params.label: Listener rule
params.help: Listener rule whose redirect action to update.

params.name: protocol
params.label: Redirect protocol
params.help: Protocol to use; #{protocol} preserves the client's incoming protocol.

operations.title: Set listener rule redirect status code
operations.summary: Choose whether this rule's redirect is permanent or temporary. Permanent redirects get cached by browsers and CDNs, even after you revert.
operations.description: Changes action.redirect.status_code between permanent (HTTP_301) and temporary (HTTP_302) on a rule whose action already redirects. Note: HTTP_301 responses are cached by browsers/CDNs and can persist in client caches even after a revert.

params.name: listener_rule
params.label: Listener rule
params.help: Listener rule whose redirect action to update.

params.name: status_code
params.label: Redirect status code
params.help: Redirect type: HTTP_301 is a permanent redirect, HTTP_302 a temporary one.

operations.title: Set listener rule redirect port
operations.summary: Set the destination port this listener rule redirects to.
operations.description: Changes action.redirect.port on a rule whose action already redirects; accepts a numeric port 1-65535 (the #{port} passthrough placeholder is not offered through this bounded form).

params.name: listener_rule
params.label: Listener rule
params.help: Listener rule whose redirect action to update.

params.name: port
params.label: Redirect port
params.help: Numeric destination port, 1-65535.

operations.title: Set listener rule weighted-forward target group weight
operations.summary: Set how much traffic share a target group gets in this rule's weighted forward, for canary or blue-green rollouts.
operations.description: Sets the weight (0-999) for a specific target group within a rule's weighted forward action, shifting the share of traffic it receives (e.g. canary / blue-green). The target group selects which member of the forward block to reweight.

params.name: listener_rule
params.label: Listener rule
params.help: Listener rule whose weighted forward action to update.

params.name: target_group
params.label: Target group
params.help: Target group within the rule's weighted forward whose weight to set.

params.name: weight
params.label: Weight
params.help: Relative weight 0-999 for this target group.

operations.title: Enable or disable listener rule weighted-forward stickiness
operations.summary: Turn target-group stickiness on or off for this rule's weighted forward. Toggling it can briefly disrupt users with an active session.
operations.description: Toggles action.forward.stickiness.enabled for a rule's weighted forward action. Turning target-group affinity on or off can disrupt in-flight user sessions.

params.name: listener_rule
params.label: Listener rule
params.help: Listener rule whose weighted forward stickiness to update.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Stickiness enabled
params.help: Enable or disable listener rule weighted-forward stickiness: true enables or disable listener rule weighted-forward stickiness, false disables it.

operations.title: Set listener rule weighted-forward stickiness duration
operations.summary: Set how long this rule's weighted-forward stickiness lasts. Shortening it a lot can drop affinity for users with an active session.
operations.description: Sets action.forward.stickiness.duration (1-604800 seconds / up to 7 days) for a rule's weighted forward action. Drastically shortening it can drop target-group affinity for active sessions.

params.name: listener_rule
params.label: Listener rule
params.help: Listener rule whose weighted forward stickiness to update.

params.name: duration_seconds
params.label: Stickiness duration (seconds)
params.help: 1-604800 seconds (up to 7 days).

operations.title: Set listener rule Cognito on-unauthenticated-request behavior
operations.summary: Set how this rule treats requests without a login: let through, block, or send to sign in. A security control; an engineer reviews it first.
operations.description: Changes action.authenticate_cognito.on_unauthenticated_request. Setting allow lets unauthenticated users through, disabling the Cognito authentication gate; deny blocks them; authenticate starts the login flow. Engineer-reviewed because it is a security-boundary control.

params.name: listener_rule
params.label: Listener rule
params.help: Listener rule whose Cognito authenticate action to update.

params.name: behavior
params.label: On unauthenticated request
params.help: How to handle an unauthenticated request: deny blocks it, allow lets it through unauthenticated, authenticate challenges the user to log in.

operations.title: Set listener rule OIDC on-unauthenticated-request behavior
operations.summary: Set how this rule treats requests without an OIDC login: allow, block, or send to sign in. An engineer reviews it first.
operations.description: Changes action.authenticate_oidc.on_unauthenticated_request. Setting allow lets unauthenticated users through, disabling the OIDC authentication gate; deny blocks them; authenticate starts the login flow. Engineer-reviewed because it is a security-boundary control.

params.name: listener_rule
params.label: Listener rule
params.help: Listener rule whose OIDC authenticate action to update.

params.name: behavior
params.label: On unauthenticated request
params.help: How to handle an unauthenticated request: deny blocks it, allow lets it through unauthenticated, authenticate challenges the user to log in.

operations.title: Create a new target group
operations.summary: Request a new target group for a backend or blue-green cutover; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it's applied.
operations.description: Create a target group for a new backend or a blue/green cutover, with the same health-check fields you can tune later. Submitting routes the complete specification to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish.

params.name: tg_name
params.label: Target group name
params.help: Up to 32 letters, numbers and dashes (an AWS limit).

params.name: protocol
params.label: Protocol
params.help: HTTPS unless the backend only speaks HTTP.

params.name: port
params.label: Backend port
params.help: The port your servers listen on.

params.name: vpc
params.label: Network (VPC)
params.help: Which network the targets live in.

params.name: target_type
params.label: Targets are
params.help: Instance for EC2 hosts; IP for addresses outside EC2. This choice is fixed once created.

params.name: health_path
params.label: Health check path
params.help: The URL the load balancer probes.

params.name: healthy_threshold
params.label: Healthy after (checks)
params.help: Consecutive successes before a target counts as healthy — the same field you can tune later.

params.name: unhealthy_threshold
params.label: Unhealthy after (checks)
params.help: Consecutive failures before a target counts as unhealthy.

params.name: interval_seconds
params.label: Check interval (seconds)
params.help: How often the load balancer probes each target.

params.name: matcher
params.label: Healthy response codes
params.help: HTTP status codes that count as healthy, like 200-399.

params.name: deregistration_delay
params.label: Drain time (seconds)
params.help: How long to let in-flight requests finish when a target is removed.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Target group name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this target group holds
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this target group.

decisions: Registering servers into this group happens in the console today — the estate does not manage registrations in Terraform
Wire it into a listener with the add-a-listener-rule action after creation

operations.title: Provision a new load balancer
operations.summary: Request a brand-new Application or Network Load Balancer; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new load balancer as a complete specification: type, internal/internet-facing, subnets, security groups and deletion protection. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review. Listeners and target groups are added afterward with the existing create actions.

params.name: lb_name
params.label: Load balancer name
params.help: Max 32 chars, alphanumeric/hyphens, no leading/trailing hyphen (doc constraint) — same rule the rename action enforces.

params.name: load_balancer_type
params.label: Load balancer type
params.help: Gateway Load Balancer is an engineer decision (specialized target-group type) — use the request-a-change catch-all for that.

params.name: internal
params.label: Internal (private)
params.help: On (internal) keeps this load balancer reachable only from inside the VPC. Off makes it internet-facing on the chosen subnets' public IPs.

params.name: subnets
params.label: Subnets
params.help: Pick subnets spanning at least two Availability Zones. For internet-facing, these must be public subnets.

params.name: security_groups
params.label: Security groups
params.help: Required for an Application Load Balancer. Pick one to five groups.

params.name: enable_deletion_protection
params.label: Deletion protection
params.help: Always on for a new load balancer — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Load balancer name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this load balancer is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this load balancer.

decisions: internal defaults to true (private, VPC-internal only); setting it false makes this load balancer internet-facing on the chosen subnets' public IPs — confirm that is intended and that those subnets are actually public before flipping it
Listeners and target groups are separate follow-up steps (the existing create-a-listener and create-a-target-group actions) — a load balancer with neither serves no traffic
Access/connection/health-check log destinations, WAF association, cross-zone load balancing and idle timeout keep their AWS defaults — tune them afterward with the existing set-* actions
